chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُؤْمِنِ وَ عَلَامَاتِهِ وَ صِفَاتِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 99 –The Momin, and his signs, and his description

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/237)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/237)
text: شِيعَتُنَا هُمُ الشَّاحِبُونَ الذَّابِلُونَ النَّاحِلُونَ الَّذِينَ إِذَا جَنَّهُمُ اللَّيْلُ اسْتَقْبَلُوهُ بِحُزْنٍ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Muhammad Bin Al Hasan Bin Za’lan, from Abu Is’haq Al Khurasany, from Amro Bin Jumi’e Al Abdy,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Our<sup>asws</sup> Shia are the pale, the withered, the slender, those, when the night covers them, they welcome it with grief’.
en.sarwar: 7. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Muhammad ibn al-Hassan ibn (za)’lan from abu Ishaq al-Khurasani from ‘Amr ibn Jumay’ al-‘Abdi from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Our Shi’a (followers) look pale, with parched (lips), slim and they receive the darkening night with sadness.”
